global.title: Vetra Studio is the builder environment for creating and managing Powerhouse package specifications
global.content:
Vetra Studio consists of two main components:

1. **Vetra Studio Drive** - A hub for developers to access, manage, and share specification documents through a visual interface. Each specification document corresponds to a module (document model, editor, data integration) that is a building block of your package.
2. **Vetra Package Library** - A registry at vetra.io/packages for storing, publishing, and forking package repositories.

Vetra Studio runs inside **Connect**, the Powerhouse host application. When you run `ph vetra --watch`, it starts a local Reactor, launches Vetra Studio, and watches for specification changes to trigger automatic code generation.

Vetra supports three modes:
- **Interactive mode** (`--interactive`): Confirmation prompts before code generation
- **Watch mode** (`--watch`): Dynamic reloading for document-models and editors
- **Standard mode**: Automatic processing with 1-second debounce
global.description: Vetra Studio Drive serves as the orchestration hub where builders assemble specifications for document models, editors, and integrations.
